title: AI data centers' massive demand for aluminum is crushing the US aluminum industry
detail:
AI data centers' massive demand for aluminum is crushing the US aluminum industry
Jake Conley · Breaking Business News Reporter
Fri, November 28, 2025 at 5:00 AM CST 8 min read
In this article:
ALI=F
CENX
GS
AA
ORCL

The boom in metal-intensive technologies like data centers and electric vehicles has made it a prime time to be in the US aluminum business.

Prices are booming. Part of that is because inside every data center are cooling units, server racks, radiators, and a litany of other pieces and parts made out of aluminum.

No wonder demand is high.

But data centers guzzle enormous amounts of power, and electricity prices are skyrocketing. In the US alone, electricity demand is expected to grow five to 10 times faster over the next 10 years than it did in the previous decade, per Bank of America.

For aluminum smelters, this is a problem. Producing aluminum is incredibly energy-intensive, and without cheap power, those smelters can't operate.

If power weren't enough of a problem, the sector is also facing pressure from overseas, where international competitors are boosting supply. Indonesia, already a major global supplier of bauxite, is steadily increasing its production of processed aluminum as it seeks to become a powerhouse, per Jefferies. China, the world's largest producer of aluminum several times over, is doing the same.

The US aluminum sector, which is nearly dormant after years of shuttering smelters, is now attempting to build out more capacity as demand is racing upward. And that demand — from data centers to power transmission equipment — has supported stock prices. For example, shares of Alcoa Corporation (AA) and Century Aluminum Company (CENX), which operate the only domestic aluminum smelters left in the US, have both recovered from the April tariff market shock to post gains so far this year.

"In a world in which we are more energy intensive ... it's going to be built with aluminum," said Charles Johnson, the president and CEO of the Aluminum Association trade group.

But just as Big Tech's data center boom is sparking new demand for the metal, the industry's insatiable desire for power — and its willingness to pay above market for it — is hampering any domestic build-out of smelting capacity and cutting into supply available for the AI arms race.

The process

Aluminum must pass through several stages to reach its final usable metal form that can be cut, bent, and shaped to form a cooling unit or battery-powered supply device. First, bauxite, an aluminum-rich mineral, must be strip-mined out of the ground. That bauxite is then converted to alumina, a purified aluminum oxide made by subjecting bauxite to a series of chemical reactions.

The final smelting process — turning alumina into aluminum by heating it up to more than 1,700 degrees Fahrenheit and then chemically separating out the material that will become metal — is extraordinarily energy-intensive. For every metric ton of produced aluminum, a smelter will consume roughly 14 megawatt-hours of electricity, enough energy to power the average US home for nearly a year and a half or drive a Tesla (TSLA) Model Y more than 50,000 miles.

title: Analyst flags 9 bargain stocks in a ‘Black Friday’-style dip
detail:
Analyst flags 9 bargain stocks in a ‘Black Friday’-style dip
Sam Boughedda
Fri, November 28, 2025 at 8:09 AM CST 2 min read
In this article:
AXON
ORCL
LYV

Investing.com -- Wolfe Research says the recent market pullback has created a “Black Friday Bargains” opportunity for investors willing to look past shaky charts and focus on solid fundamentals.

Analyst Rob Ginsberg writes: “Who doesn’t love a good deal?” Adding that while strong performers have their place, “sometimes it pays to go bottom fishing.”

To build the list, Wolfe Research screened for stocks under its coverage that carry an Outperform rating, offer “greater than 20% upside to the price target,” and are “oversold technically, and trading at or above identifiable support.”

The result, Ginsberg says, is a list of nine names with robust fundamentals that “have gone on sale of late.”

Axon is approaching “support… at $500,” and while oversold conditions haven’t mattered yet, Wolfe says it’s “worth a shot with a tight $500 stop.”

Meanwhile, Live Nation sits at a “30% drawdown,” with Wolfe warning that if it fails to stabilise at $120, it could break below $110.

Palo Alto Networks offers what Wolfe calls one of its “favorite types of setups: oversold within an uptrend,” with $180 a key level.

Oracle, now “45% off its Sept high,” is nearing major support, though Wolfe cautions: “Lose $180 and we’re cutting our losses.”

Uber has hit six-month lows, but Wolfe points to “significant price memory at $80.”

DoorDash is “trying to find its footing above $180,” a level that previously acted as a consolidation zone.

The firm added that Formula One Group faces a decisive moment at the “$95–93” support band, while Zscaler is showing “exactly what we want to see,” holding its 200-day moving average.

Arista Networks rounds out the list; Wolfe calls it “a bargain we’re buying,” with “$140+ in play over the near term if relief persists.”

title: Oracle Faces Rising Debt Risk as Morgan Stanley Flags Credit Market Concerns
detail:
Oracle Faces Rising Debt Risk as Morgan Stanley Flags Credit Market Concerns
Faizan Farooque
Fri, November 28, 2025 at 5:47 AM CST 1 min read
In this article:
ORCL

This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Oracle (ORCL, Financials) is drawing increased scrutiny in credit markets as borrowing tied to its artificial intelligence and data-center buildout accelerates. Analysts at Morgan Stanley said five-year credit-default swap costs climbed to 1.25 percentage points in November, the highest since 2022, and could move above 1.5 points next year without more detail on the company's long-term funding strategy.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 6 Warning Signs with ORCL.

Is ORCL fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

The shift comes as Oracle raises substantial capital to support AI infrastructure. The company recently issued $18 billion in bonds and has been linked to large financing packages for new data-center developments, including an $18 billion loan in New Mexico and a $38 billion loan tied to sites in Texas and Wisconsin. Morgan Stanley said the rapid increase in borrowing has prompted more investors to hedge exposure, pushing CDS prices higher.

Attention now turns to Oracle's second-quarter fiscal 2026 earnings on Dec. 15. Analysts expect the company to report $1.64 in earnings per share on revenue of $16.20 billion. Morgan Stanley said the call will be a key moment for Oracle to outline how it intends to fund large-scale projects, including its Stargate initiative.

title: Oracle Draws Bank Interest for $38 Billion in New Funding to Support OpenAI Sites
detail:
Oracle Draws Bank Interest for $38 Billion in New Funding to Support OpenAI Sites
Faizan Farooque
Fri, November 28, 2025 at 5:23 AM CST 1 min read
In this article:
ORCL

This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Oracle (ORCL, Financials) is holding preliminary discussions with several banks about a loan package of roughly $38 billion to help build more data-center sites for OpenAI, according to a Financial Times report citing people familiar with the matter. Reuters has not confirmed the details, but the talks reflect how quickly capital requirements are rising across the AI infrastructure sector.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 6 Warning Signs with ORCL.

Is ORCL fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

The potential financing would back projects developed with Vantage Data Centers, a partner in earlier efforts to expand capacity for OpenAI workloads. Banks have shown continued interest in supporting these buildouts, even as the size of the commitments grows. Oracle has already raised significant debt this year, including an $18 billion bond sale and multibillion-dollar project loans tied to planned facilities in New Mexico, Texas and Wisconsin.

If the new package moves forward, it would rank among the largest AI-related financings so far. Investors will look to Oracle's Dec. 15 earnings call for guidance on how the company intends to manage the funding needs associated with its expanding data-center pipeline and long-term AI strategy.

title: Calculating The Fair Value Of Oracle Corporation (NYSE:ORCL)
detail:
Calculating The Fair Value Of Oracle Corporation (NYSE:ORCL)
Simply Wall St
Fri, November 28, 2025 at 5:00 AM CST 6 min read
In this article:
ORCL
Key Insights

Using the 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity, Oracle fair value estimate is US$210

Current share price of US$205 suggests Oracle is potentially trading close to its fair value

Analyst price target for ORCL is US$342, which is 63% above our fair value estimate

In this article we are going to estimate the intrinsic value of Oracle Corporation (NYSE:ORCL) by estimating the company's future cash flows and discounting them to their present value. Our analysis will employ the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model. There's really not all that much to it, even though it might appear quite complex.

We would caution that there are many ways of valuing a company and, like the DCF, each technique has advantages and disadvantages in certain scenarios. For those who are keen learners of equity analysis, the Simply Wall St analysis model here may be something of interest to you.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

The Calculation

We are going to use a two-stage DCF model, which, as the name states, takes into account two stages of growth. The first stage is generally a higher growth period which levels off heading towards the terminal value, captured in the second 'steady growth' period. To start off with, we need to estimate the next ten years of cash flows. Where possible we use analyst estimates, but when these aren't available we extrapolate the previous free cash flow (FCF) from the last estimate or reported value. We assume companies with shrinking free cash flow will slow their rate of shrinkage, and that companies with growing free cash flow will see their growth rate slow, over this period. We do this to reflect that growth tends to slow more in the early years than it does in later years.

A DCF is all about the idea that a dollar in the future is less valuable than a dollar today, so we need to discount the sum of these future cash flows to arrive at a present value estimate:

In recent weeks, Energy Transfer announced several major natural gas supply agreements with leading AI data center operators, including Oracle and Fermi, while approving billions of dollars in new pipeline and infrastructure projects funded through 2026.

This wave of long-term, fee-based contracts and expansion initiatives positions Energy Transfer to directly tap rising energy demand from the rapid growth of data centers, a trend increasingly linked to the accelerating adoption of artificial intelligence.

We'll explore how these new data center contracts may influence Energy Transfer's future growth prospects and long-term earnings outlook.

This technology could replace computers: discover 28 stocks that are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Energy Transfer Investment Narrative Recap

To be an Energy Transfer shareholder, you need to believe in the essential role of fee-based, long-term natural gas infrastructure in powering the growth of AI-driven data centers and US energy exports, while accepting the business’s exposure to pipeline buildouts and changing regulatory dynamics. The recent wave of major data center supply agreements provides valuable contract visibility and is a clear positive, but the most important short-term catalyst remains timely permitting and execution on large-scale pipeline projects; the biggest risk is that delays, cost overruns, or regulatory hang-ups could crimp cash flows and stall planned distribution growth. At this stage, while the new contracts bolster the growth story, they don't materially alter that fundamental risk, so keeping an eye on project execution remains essential.

Among recent announcements, Energy Transfer’s approval of $4.6 billion in organic expansion projects for 2025, focused on new processing and pipeline capacity, directly ties into those long-term demand drivers from data centers and global LNG. These projects are potentially incremental to future contracted cash flows, but also underscore the scale and complexity of execution that underpins the company’s near-term outlook.

title: Is Oracle Corporation (ORCL) One of The AI Stocks Analysts are Betting on?
detail:
Is Oracle Corporation (ORCL) One of The AI Stocks Analysts are Betting on?
Neha Gupta
Fri, November 28, 2025 at 12:21 AM CST 2 min read
In this article:
ORCL
OPAI.PVT

Oracle Corporation (NYSE:ORCL) is one of the AI stocks analysts are betting on. On November 26, HSBC maintained its Buy rating on Oracle Corporation (NYSE:ORCL) with a $382 price target, highlighting the company’s efforts to explore funding options for cloud infrastructure expansion.

Is Oracle Corporation (ORCL) One of The AI Stocks Analysts are Betting on?

While Oracle has not yet detailed a specific plan, HSBC noted that capital-intensive projects could use structures such as special purpose vehicles or joint ventures to limit balance sheet risk. The firm also pointed to Oracle’s $500 billion in remaining performance obligations, strong revenue visibility, and focus on maintaining its investment-grade rating as it competes with AWS and Azure for market share.

On the same day, Deutsche Bank reiterated its Buy rating with a $375 price target, emphasizing Oracle’s partnership with OpenAI amid recent share price weakness and questions around AI capacity commitments. Deutsche Bank noted that excluding OpenAI-related revenues and expenses could reduce EPS by $4 to $17 and free cash flow by $10 billion to $31 billion by FY2030.

Even so, the bank argued that the market is undervaluing Oracle’s OpenAI business and that under conservative assumptions, the company could still generate $15 EPS and $26 billion in free cash flow. Analysts expect Oracle’s December 15 earnings report to provide more clarity on its financial outlook and AI strategy.

Oracle Corporation (NYSE:ORCL) integrates Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) into its cloud infrastructure, a dedicated AI data platform, and its enterprise software applications to automate processes, provide predictive insights, and enhance decision-making across various business functions.

While we acknowledge the potential of ORCL as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: US stocks rise on last trading day of November
detail:
US stocks rise on last trading day of November
Trader Edward Curran works on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange, Monday, Nov. 24, 2025. (AP Photo/Richard Drew) · Associated Press
Associated Press
Thu, November 27, 2025 at 11:44 PM CST 3 min read
In this article:
^GSPC
BTC-USD
^DJI
^IXIC
COIN

U.S. stocks rose in morning trading as Wall Street puts the wraps on a volatile month.

The S&P 500 rose 0.4% and needs to hold that gain to avoid its first down month since April. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 282 points, and the Nasdaq gained 0.5% as of 11:10 a.m. ET.

Coinbase Global added 4.3% as bitcoin rose above $92,000 after dropping to around $81,000 last week. The world's most popular cryptocurrency is still well below its all-time high of around $125,000 set in early October.

Most tech stocks posted gains, with Meta Platforms rising 1.5% and Micron Technology adding 2.4%. But Nvidia, the market's most valuable stock, fell 1.4% and is headed for a double-digit loss for the month. Oracle another high-flyer that struggled this month, fell 2.1%.

Wall Street is operating on an abbreviated schedule Friday after being closed for the Thanksgiving holiday. Stock trading closes at 1 p.m. ET.

Earlier, futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average, S&P 500 and Nasdaq were halted for hours due to a technical issue at the Chicago Mercantile Exchange. CME said the problem was tied to an outage at a CyrusOne data center.

After slumping earlier this month as investors worried that many of the tech stocks that were propelled higher by the frenzy over artificial intelligence had gotten too expensive, the market has risen for four straight trading sessions on hopes the Federal Reserve will again cut interest rates at its meeting next month.

Recent comments from Federal Reserve officials have given traders more confidence the central bank will again cut interest rates at its meeting that ends Dec. 10. Traders are betting on a nearly 87% probability that the Fed will cut next month, according to data from CME Group.

The central bank, which has already cut rates twice this year in hopes of shoring up the slowing job market, is facing an increasingly difficult decision on interest rates as inflation rises and the job market slows. Cutting interest rates further could help support the economy as employment weakens, but it could also fuel inflation. The latest round of corporate earnings reports was mostly positive, but economic data has been mixed.

The minutes of the Fed’s most recent meeting in October indicate there are likely to be strong divisions among policymakers about the Fed’s next step.

Investors also had their eye on retail stocks as they wait to see if shoppers rushed to take advantage of the annual Black Friday sales event. Macy's rose 0.6% and Kohl's gained 2% but Dick's Sporting Goods dropped 1.4%. Among specialty retailers, Abercrombie & Fitch rose 4.4% and American Eagle Outfitters gained 0.7%.

Story Continues

Customer Relationship Management (CRM) is a technology-driven strategy and system that enables businesses to manage relationships with existing and prospective customers. CRM software centralizes client data, such as purchase history, communication records, and preferences, to improve sales, marketing, and customer service activities. CRM helps businesses enhance lead management, customer engagement, and retention by combining analytics, automation, and real-time communication capabilities. It facilitates decision-making through data-driven insights, resulting in more personalized experiences and better partnerships. CRM is widely utilized across sectors to improve operational efficiency, increase customer satisfaction, and drive revenue development by aligning corporate processes with changing consumer demands and expectations.

The North American CRM market is being pushed by fast digital transformation across industries, with organizations preferring customer-centric approaches. Cloud-based CRM adoption is increasing due to its flexibility, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. The expansion of e-commerce and mobile commerce is driving demand for integrated customer interaction solutions.

The artificial intelligence of things (AIoT) market represents a crucial segment within the broader digital transformation and industrial automation ecosystem. AIoT solutions integrate artificial intelligence capabilities directly into IoT devices, enabling enhanced data processing, predictive analytics, and autonomous operations. Continuous advancements in machine learning algorithms, edge computing infrastructure, and sensor technologies are driving the adoption of AIoT across sectors such as manufacturing, healthcare, smart cities, and transportation.

The market benefits from innovations including federated learning, AI-enabled cybersecurity frameworks, and real-time analytics platforms that reduce latency and improve system responsiveness. Furthermore, the integration of AIoT solutions is facilitating predictive maintenance, energy optimization, and improved user experiences in consumer and enterprise environments. The artificial intelligence of things (AIoT) market is bolstered by sustained investments in research and development, aimed at enhancing device interoperability, security protocols, and scalable deployment models.

Global Artificial Intelligence of Things (AIoT) Market Lifecycle Stage

The artificial intelligence of things (AIoT) market is currently in a rapid growth phase, driven by increasing adoption of intelligent connected devices and government initiatives supporting digital infrastructure modernization. Market maturity varies by region, with North America and Asia-Pacific leading in deployment and innovation, supported by robust technology ecosystems and regulatory frameworks. The U.S., in particular, continues to invest heavily in AIoT research, infrastructure development, and strategic partnerships, fostering an environment conducive to market expansion.

Collaborative efforts among technology vendors, telecom operators, and industrial enterprises are accelerating the development of integrated AIoT platforms. Regulatory considerations around data privacy and cybersecurity play a significant role in shaping market dynamics, ensuring the safe and ethical deployment of AIoT technologies. Over the forecast period, the artificial intelligence of things (AIoT) market is projected to sustain strong growth, propelled by advancements in 5G connectivity, edge AI, and increasing enterprise digitalization initiatives globally.

Key Points in This Article:

Microsoft is dedicating significant capex to AI and cloud infrastructure in order to compete with other tech firms.

Microsoft’s gaming segment grew 44% last year, providing significant revenue to complement its software, cloud and AI business lines.

Shares of Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) lost 1.52% over the past five trading sessions after losing 1.48% the five prior. That brings MSFT's year-to-date gain to 15.99%, including a nearly 37% gain since its year-to-date low on April 8.

When the Magnificent Seven member reported Q3 earnings on Oct. 29, shares fell despite beating on EPS and revenue. The company announced earnings of $3.72 per share versus analysts' expectations of $3.67, and quarterly revenue of $77.67 billion versus analysts' expectations of $75.33 billion.

On Oct. 1, the company announced that it was increasing its Xbox Game Pass subscription by 50%. In its last fiscal year, Microsoft saw more than 8% of revenue derived from its gaming segment, which now boasts 50 million monthly active subscribers and nearly $5 billion in YoY revenue.

In June, it was reported that the company will be expanding its AI and cloud investments in Switzerland, committing $400 million to expand its data center infrastructure in the European nation. The additional capacity is expected to support more than 50,000 current customers and expand the availability of AI services for more sectors, including health care, finance government. Microsoft is capitalizing on its Azure platform’s momentum as revenue jumped 39% in FY25 Q4, driven by AI services.

Microsoft's decision in May fire 6,000 employees — or 3% of its workforce — signals the tech giant is serious about cost discipline amid economic uncertainty. With analysts eyeing sustained cloud demand, 24/7 Wall St. conducted analysis to explore whether Microsoft can maintain its upward trajectory and drive long-term growth.

Why Invest in Microsoft

Microsoft navigates challenges, but remains a prime investment due to its AI and cloud dominance. Third-quarter earnings showcased robust demand for its Intelligent Cloud segment, though tariff risks linger. Microsoft’s $80 billion cash reserve fuels its $80 billion investments in cloud and AI infrastructure, with over half in the U.S.

Its Microsoft 365 Copilot, adopted by over 70% of Fortune 500 firms, drives productivity revenue, positioning Microsoft to capture the AI market’s 37% compounded annual growth predicted through 2030. Similarly, partnerships with Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) for multi-cloud solutions bolster its competitiveness against Amazon's (NASDAQ:AMZN) AWS.
